howItWorks.intro: Finding the tempo of a song is as simple as tapping along to the beat. This tool measures the time between your taps and calculates the BPM.
rules: Tap the button in time with the beat — the more taps, the more accurate the reading
At least 4 taps are needed for a reliable BPM reading
The BPM auto-resets if you stop tapping for more than 3 seconds
howItWorks.outro: You can also manually enter a BPM to convert it to milliseconds per beat, which is useful for setting delay and reverb times in music production.

faq.question: How many taps do I need for an accurate BPM?
faq.answer: At least 4 taps will give you a reasonable estimate, but 8-16 taps provide much better accuracy. The more taps you make, the more the random timing variations average out. Most musicians tap for 8-10 beats for a reliable reading.

faq.question: What BPM is common for different music genres?
faq.answer: Hip-hop: 80-115 BPM. Pop: 100-130 BPM. House: 120-130 BPM. Techno: 125-150 BPM. Drum & Bass: 160-180 BPM. Dubstep: 140 BPM (half-time feel). Metal: 100-160 BPM. Classical varies widely from 40-200+ BPM.

faq.question: What does BPM stand for?
faq.answer: BPM stands for Beats Per Minute. It measures the tempo or speed of music. A BPM of 60 means one beat per second. A BPM of 120 means two beats per second. It's the universal standard for describing musical tempo.

faq.question: How do I convert BPM to milliseconds?
faq.answer: Divide 60,000 by the BPM. For example, 120 BPM = 60,000 / 120 = 500 milliseconds per beat. This conversion is essential for setting delay times, sequencer timing, and synchronizing effects in music production.

faq.question: Why does the BPM reset after I stop tapping?
faq.answer: The tool resets after 3 seconds of no taps to ensure fresh, accurate readings. If the gap between taps is too long, the intervals would skew the average and give an incorrectly slow BPM reading.

faq.question: Can I use keyboard keys to tap?
faq.answer: Yes! You can press the spacebar or Enter key to tap instead of clicking the button. This is often easier for getting accurate timing since you can keep your eyes on the music source while tapping.
